title: Grammar code generator (tsgo-generate)
description:
Build the tool that compiles tree-sitter grammars to Go packages.

Deliverables:
- cmd/tsgo-generate/main.go: CLI that reads grammar JSON (src/grammar.json) + node-types.json (src/node-types.json) from a tree-sitter grammar repo and produces a Go package.
- Generated output includes: parse tables as Go slice literals, lex function as Go for/switch state machine, keyword lex function, symbol metadata (names, visibility), field maps, alias sequences, LexMode array, external scanner stub (interface + factory placeholder).
- Lex function generation: translate C DFA states to Go switch cases. Use equivalence classes with O(1) ASCII lookup table (128-byte array) + binary search for Unicode. BFS state numbering for cache locality.
- Validate by generating JSON grammar and comparing output to hand-compiled version from task 1.
- Generate Go and JavaScript grammars to test robustness with larger/more complex grammars.

Tests:
- Generate JSON grammar, parse files, verify identical output to hand-compiled version
- Generate Go grammar, parse Go standard library files, compare S-expressions with C tree-sitter
- Generate JavaScript grammar, parse JS files, compare with C tree-sitter
- Test equivalence class table correctness
- Test lex function generation for edge cases (Unicode characters, large ADVANCE_MAP entries)

Reference: docs/design.md §3, docs/addendum-lexer-performance.md Phase 0
